item_name: Maintaining Asset Library Consistency at Scale
definition_origin: Asset library consistency = the discipline of locking visual/audio decisions into versioned, machine-readable, single-source-of-truth artifacts (style guide, design tokens, character bible, prompt library, naming convention) so every asset shipped 6 months from now matches every asset shipped today. Borrowed from animation-studio character bibles, brand-agency style guides, and software design tokens (W3C draft spec). For solo dev: prevents drift as scope grows from 10 to 500 assets.
core_purpose: At Norman's REFLECTIVE level, consistency is the foundation of brand recognition — users trust apps that feel internally coherent. At BEHAVIORAL level, consistent stroke widths/corner radii train muscle memory (users predict where buttons go because everything follows the system). At VISCERAL level, inconsistency reads as 'amateur' immediately. For a solo dev the library is force-multiplier: 1 hour locking down tokens saves 50 hours of inconsistent ad-hoc decisions later. Without it AI-generated assets diverge wildly between sessions.
when_needed: Mandatory once asset count crosses ~20 unique pieces or once a second contributor (human or AI agent) joins. Even before that, locking down tokens.json + archetype-spec.md prevents personal drift across sessions. Critical for anything shipping notifications/mascot/illustrations across multiple surfaces. Less critical for one-screen utility apps with no brand layer, but even there a 5-color palette lock saves hours.
format_options: tokens.json (W3C-aligned design tokens; the source of truth — primitives + semantic + component tiers). YAML alternative for human readability. Style Dictionary output: CSS vars, Tailwind theme, RN StyleSheet, iOS Swift, Android XML — generated FROM tokens.json. Figma Variables (native since 2023, mirrors tokens.json structure). Penpot tokens (native since 1.19, free + open-source). character-bible.md or .pdf — human-readable mascot reference (proportions sheet, expression chart, do/don't, voice). prompt-library.md — versioned reusable AI prompt fragments. asset-naming.md — naming convention spec. Folder structure (semver versioned with git): /assets/{icons,illustrations,animations,audio,3d}/.
file_size_performance: tokens.json itself <100KB even for large systems. Generated outputs (CSS, RN StyleSheet) typically <50KB after Style Dictionary. Character bible PDF can be 5-20MB but is internal-only. Storage cost trivial; bandwidth cost zero (tokens are build-time not runtime). The performance cost of NOT having a library is huge: ad-hoc assets bloat bundle, duplicate colors fragment cache, inconsistent compression wastes bytes.
free_tools: Figma (free, free Variables for design tokens — primary tool for solo). Penpot (FREE open-source, native design tokens since v1.19, GitHub-syncable, self-host OR penpot.app cloud). Tokens Studio for Figma (free tier; export to JSON, sync to GitHub, transform via Style Dictionary). Style Dictionary (Amazon's free open-source build tool — JSON tokens to platform code). Theo (Salesforce equivalent, less popular now). GitHub for version control (free for public; free private for solo). Storybook / Ladle (free; component + asset gallery for visual review). Chromatic free tier (visual regression testing). Figma Tokens (free Figma plugin, predecessor to Tokens Studio). Specify (free tier, design-tokens-as-a-service). Supernova free tier (design system documentation). The free combo: Figma + Tokens Studio + Style Dictionary + GitHub covers 95% of solo-dev needs at $0/mo.
paid_tools: STARTER ($10-15/mo): Figma Professional $15/seat/mo (unlimited library files, version history, branching). PRO ($20-30/mo): Tokens Studio Pro $11/mo (advanced sync workflows). Supernova $25/mo (auto-generated docs site). Specify Pro $20/mo. ENTERPRISE ($50+/mo): zeroheight $30-100/mo per editor (best-in-class design system documentation site). Frontify $50+/mo (brand portal). Skip all paid tools as solo dev — the free stack is genuinely complete.
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro maintains LIBRARY DISCIPLINE: (1) Token audit: paste tokens.json + a new component spec, ask 'does this component use only existing tokens? if not, list the new tokens it would require and propose whether to add or refactor.' (2) Naming convention enforcement: 'audit this filename list against asset-naming.md spec; flag any violations and propose corrected names'. (3) Character bible expansion: 'given mascot {NAME} with archetype {ARCHETYPE} and current expression sheet (16 expressions), generate the next 4 expressions with detailed visual notes (eyes, mouth, posture, accent color use)'. (4) Prompt-library refactor: 'consolidate these 12 ad-hoc Imagen prompts into 3 templates with placeholder variables; ensure all reference archetype-spec.md tokens'. (5) Cross-platform translation: 'convert these Figma Variables to Style Dictionary input format then to Tailwind config + RN StyleSheet + Swift'. (6) NotebookLM with all design docs (archetype-spec, tokens, character-bible, slop-anti-tokens) as a queryable knowledge source — 'is this asset on-brand?'. (7) Imagen 4 for character bible expansion locked to existing reference image.
workflow_steps: Step 1 — Lock primitives (1 hr): write tokens.json with primitives layer (color/spacing/font/radius/shadow raw values). Step 2 — Add semantic layer (1 hr): map primitives to semantic tokens (color.primary = primitive.brand.500). Step 3 — Generate platform outputs (30 min): Style Dictionary build → CSS, Tailwind, RN StyleSheet, Swift, XML. Wire to packages/shared/src/tokens/. Step 4 — Build character bible (3-4 hr if mascot exists): proportion sheet (1 page), 16-expression chart (1 page), 12-pose chart (1 page), do/don't (1 page), voice + tone (1 page). Step 5 — Define naming convention (30 min): pattern like {category}-{archetype}-{name}-{variant}-{state}.{ext} e.g. mascot-jester-duo-pose01-cheering.svg. Step 6 — Build prompt library (2 hr): /design/prompt-library/ with templates per asset type, each referencing tokens. Step 7 — Set up Storybook (2 hr): visual gallery of every asset for human review. Step 8 — Wire CI checks (1 hr): pre-commit hook that runs SVGO, validates filenames, fails on undefined-token usage. Step 9 — Document (1 hr): README in /design/ explaining the system. Step 10 — Quarterly audit (1 hr/quarter): check drift, retire unused assets, update bible.
ai_prompt_templates: Token audit: 'Given tokens.json: {PASTE_TOKENS}. Audit this asset: {ASSET_SPEC}. Does it only use existing tokens? List violations and propose either token additions OR asset refactoring.' Character bible expansion: 'Mascot {NAME}, archetype {ARCHETYPE}, current pose sheet has {N} poses. Generate {NEW_POSE} with: head angle, eye direction, mouth shape, hand position, body lean, accent color use, emotional intent, do/don't notes. Maintain proportions (head: body ratio = {RATIO}, signature features: {LIST}).' Prompt library refactor: 'Refactor these {N} ad-hoc Imagen prompts into a single parameterized template. Identify variables; ensure prompt references {ARCHETYPE_TOKENS}; produce a final template with {{PLACEHOLDERS}} and 3 example fills.' Naming validation: 'Validate these filenames against pattern: {category}-{archetype}-{name}-{variant}-{state}.{ext}. List violations and corrected names.'
claude_code_integration: Repo structure: /packages/shared/src/tokens/{primitives.json,semantic.json,components.json}; /packages/shared/src/tokens-build/ (generated outputs, gitignored or published); /design/{archetype-spec.md, character-bible.md, prompt-library/, asset-naming.md, slop-anti-tokens.md}; /apps/mobile/assets/{icons,illustrations,animations,audio,3d}/. Pre-commit: lint-staged with SVGO + token-validator script. Style Dictionary build script in package.json: 'tokens:build': 'style-dictionary build'. Test: tokens.test.ts validates JSON schema. Have Claude Code generate type-safe asset import files: a tokens.ts with strongly-typed export so wrong colors fail at compile time. Add Storybook with one story per asset type. PR template includes 'have you updated tokens.json?' checkbox. Wire a /scripts/audit-assets.ts that scans for hardcoded colors / fonts / shadows not in tokens.
emotional_design_integration: Library consistency is itself a REFLECTIVE-level brand attribute — users perceive 'this brand has its act together' from coherence alone. Per archetype the library shape differs: SAGE/RULER libraries are SMALL and DEEP — few assets, deeply considered, restrained palette. JESTER/CREATOR libraries are LARGER and EXPRESSIVE — many variations, multi-color, more expressions. CAREGIVER libraries emphasize WARM CONSISTENCY — same brush feel everywhere. MAGICIAN libraries lock LUMINOUS REFINEMENT — gradients used identically every time, never randomly. Library size correlates with archetype: a Sage app might ship 20 assets total; a Jester app might ship 500. The character bible's 16-expression sheet for a Caregiver mascot will skew heavily toward soft empathetic emotions; for Outlaw toward defiance/confidence/rebellion.
specific_examples: Linear Design System (https://linear.app/method) — restrained, deeply consistent, public. GitHub Primer (https://primer.style/) — open-source design system with tokens.json layered (primitives → functional → component). Stripe Connect docs (https://stripe.com/connect) — illustration system locked to single illustrator's style. Headspace Brand Standards (https://standards.site/case-studies/headspace/) — public asset library. Atlassian Design System (https://atlassian.design/) — token architecture is public and copy-able. Salesforce Lightning (https://www.lightningdesignsystem.com/) — origin of Theo / Style Dictionary. Adobe Spectrum (https://spectrum.adobe.com/) — multi-platform token export reference. Polaris by Shopify (https://polaris.shopify.com/) — open-source. For mascot bibles: Duolingo's Duo bible is public-ish (https://design.duolingo.com/) — character voice and visual rules documented. Mailchimp's brand bible (https://ux.mailchimp.com/patterns/illustration) — illustration system with rules.
anti_ai_slop_traps: (1) Generating new assets in EVERY session without referencing tokens — drift happens silently and accumulates. (2) Letting AI propose new colors instead of FORCING the prompt to use the existing palette. (3) Skipping naming convention — folder becomes mascot-final.png, mascot-final-v2.png, mascot-FINAL-USE-THIS-ONE.png. (4) Storing master files in Figma cloud only without git backup. (5) Character bible drift — new pose generated last week doesn't match proportions of poses generated 6 months ago. Counter: every new asset prompt MUST start with 'reference existing token: {TOKEN_LIST}', 'reference existing pose-sheet: {URL}'. Commit token validation as CI gate so PR fails if undefined token used. Lock the character bible image as the FIRST input to every Imagen reference-locked generation.
common_pitfalls: (1) Skipping the semantic-token layer (jumping primitives to components) — leads to thrash when palette changes. (2) Over-engineering: 200-token system before shipping anything. Solo dev: start with 30 tokens, expand. (3) Hardcoded values in code that bypass tokens — wire ESLint or audit script to forbid raw hex/rgb in style files. (4) Not generating character bible for mascot before drawing 50 mascot variations — proportions drift. (5) Designing in Figma but never exporting to repo — designers' Figma is divorced from devs' code. (6) Naming convention drift between contributors — lock it in CI. (7) Forgetting to version the bible itself — character bible v1 vs v2 needs git tags. (8) Storing assets in app binary that should be CDN-streamed; library plan should include stream/bundle decisions. (9) Treating tokens.json as one-and-done — needs quarterly audit as system grows.
accessibility_notes: Tokens.json should encode contrast pairs (color.text.on-primary tested 4.5:1 AA). Lock minimum touch-target size token (44pt iOS / 48dp Android). Reduce-motion variants of every Lottie pinned in library. Alt-text snippets stored alongside illustration assets in i18n keys (illustration.empty-state.alt = 'A friendly character looking at an empty box'). Color-blind safe palette validation in token build step (use chroma.js WCAG check).
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: tokens generate to RN StyleSheet (no CSS); icon set needs 1x/2x/3x exports; fonts need explicit font family registration; respect system Dynamic Type. Web: tokens generate to CSS custom properties; icon set is single SVG sprite; fonts via @font-face or Google Fonts CDN; respect prefers-reduced-motion. Library can be SHARED via /packages/shared/tokens/ with platform-specific generators consuming the same primitive layer.
sources: [Penpot 1.19 design tokens release](https://penpot.app/blog/7-reasons-penpot-is-more-than-just-a-figma-alternative/) — open-source native design tokens. [Tokens Studio for Figma](https://tokens.studio/) — multi-platform token sync. [Style Dictionary on GitHub (Amazon)](https://github.com/amzn/style-dictionary) — source-of-truth build tool. [Design Tokens W3C Community Group spec](https://www.designtokens.org/) — emerging standard. [GitHub Primer Design System](https://primer.style/) — open token architecture. [Atlassian Design System token architecture](https://atlassian.design/foundations/tokens) — three-tier reference. [Linear Design System](https://linear.app/method) — restrained discipline. [Atomic Design / Naming Conventions Figma](https://www.figma.com/community/file/935960100615214367/atomic-design-naming-conventions) — naming pattern reference. [Atomic Design in Practice (Mar 2026 Medium)](https://medium.com/@atnoforuiuxdesigning/atomic-design-in-practice-how-to-structure-your-figma-files-like-a-design-system-engineer-8682bcb14271) — current best practices. [Duolingo Design](https://design.duolingo.com/) — character bible reference.
